title: cuscuta planiflora subsp. papillosa
intro: Cuscuta planiflora subsp. papillosa, also known as Dodder, is a species of parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting itself to the vascular tissue of its host plant. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cuscuta planiflora subsp. papillosa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation, making it a valuable remedy for conditions such as arthritis and anemia. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, or made into capsules or tinctures to be used as a supplement.
